summary.text:
Description of a single component in a shape an LLM can reason
about — name, purpose, props, and an optional concrete example.

An array of these is passed to the LLM so it knows what components
are available and how to use them. Produced by
; this package only owns the destination shape.
The component name as registered in the Puck config (e.g.

Human-readable description of what the component does and when
to use it. Forwarded verbatim into the LLM prompt — write it
as guidance to a collaborator.

Optional concrete example of a valid prop bag. The LLM uses
this as a one-shot example of how the fields should look when
populated; leaving it off falls back to zero-shot from the
field descriptions alone.

Description of a single prop on a component, in a shape an LLM can
reason about.

(Phase 3) produces an
array of these from a Puck

field descriptor. The AI
copilot plugin then feeds them into its system prompt so the model
knows which props it can set and what shape each one takes.

Recursive:

AI
contract, so it is deferred to the next contract major; until then,

own the per-type invariants
at runtime (Zod).

A single issue produced by validating an LLM's response against

for display in a dev console or a toast.

). Empty string means

The descriptor every exporter plugin contributes to Studio.

)
is performed exactly once by the export pipeline before any format
runs, ensuring every exporter sees the same input regardless of
how the page was authored.

Format authors should treat the

argument as deeply read-only.
Mutating it has no effect on the runtime and may break other
exporters in the same pipeline.

File extension (without the leading dot) for downloads in this
format (e.g.

).

The runtime rejects duplicate ids at

time. Convention: lowercase, hyphen-separated, no namespace
prefix — host apps refer to formats by id in their UI.

Human-readable label surfaced in export menus and dialogs
(e.g.
Optional i18n message key for the label, resolved by the chrome via

at render — so the export menu localizes
with the active locale. When present it wins;

is the
missing-key fallback. Plugins contribute the key via
MIME type for the exported content (e.g.

).

Used by host apps to set the correct

type at download

header when sending

Produce the serialized output for a page.

Receives a normalized

— always async so the contract is uniform regardless of
whether a given format happens to run synchronously.
The normalized page IR. Deeply read-only.
Optional runtime context for additive export hooks.

for an example of how to parameterize a

for
formats that opt into asset-URL rewriting (review finding TS-c).

is the runtime's registration-ordered resolver list
(sourced from

): a format consults each
resolver in order and stops at the first that returns a non-null
rewrite. Absent or empty ⇒ no rewriting is applied; how a format then
treats a reference no resolver handles is its own policy — the bundled
HTML / React exporters omit unresolved
warning rather than passing them through.
The return value of a successful

call.

See

. Host apps may surface these in a

A single diagnostic emitted by an exporter.

Warnings are advisory — they do not abort the export. Host apps

Human-readable warning message suitable for developer-facing

the warning is attached to.

editor.
The root IR document — the single argument passed to every

. This package
declares the shape; it does not implement the transformation.

Top-level asset manifest. Every asset referenced anywhere in
the tree should appear here exactly once; node-scoped

entries are an optimization for
exporters that process nodes independently.

The root node of the page tree.

Exporters walk this recursively to produce output.

Schema version of this IR document.

Literal
for the initial contract. A future

would be
a breaking change to IR shape and requires a migration shim in

. String (not number) to avoid JSON
floating-point surprises and to match the convention used by
most serialized IR schemas.

An out-of-band resource referenced by the page — images, videos,
fonts, stylesheets, inline scripts, etc.

Assets are listed both at the root of

(so exporters
can emit a manifest or preload block in one pass) and, optionally,
on individual

s (so a node-scoped exporter can
resolve its own dependencies without walking the whole tree).

The same asset may appear in both places — exporters are expected
to deduplicate by

Stable, unique identifier for this asset within the IR.

Typically a hash of the URL or a caller-provided slug. Exporters
use this for deduplication when the same asset is referenced by
multiple nodes.

The asset's category.

Closed union — extending this list is a breaking change to the
IR contract. Use

for anything that doesn't fit the
named categories rather than introducing an ad-hoc string.

Free-form metadata the producer wants to pass through to
consumers (e.g.

for an image).

Exporters should treat unknown keys as forward-compatible — a
missing key must not cause a hard failure.

Absolute or root-relative URL pointing at the asset.

Exporters may rewrite this (e.g. to a CDN or a hashed filename)
during serialization — the IR shape does not constrain the
scheme.

Page-level metadata — title, description, and timestamps the
exporter may surface in

, a feed, or a manifest.

All fields are optional so minimal IR snapshots can be produced
without forcing the caller to synthesize values they don't have.

Timestamps are ISO 8601 strings (not

instances) so the IR
remains JSON-serializable without a custom replacer.
Optional ISO 8601 timestamp of page creation.
Optional ISO 8601 timestamp of most-recent page update.

A single node in the page IR tree.

Structurally recursive: every node may carry a

array of
further nodes, mirroring the component tree the author laid out in
the editor. Leaf nodes omit

entirely rather than setting
it to an empty array (the distinction is not semantically
meaningful, but omitting the key keeps serialized IR snapshots
smaller).

Field caps are runtime-enforced contracts (not type-level
constraints) — the matching Zod schema lives in

's section
checker. Keeping caps off the type surface preserves the
"zero runtime code" rule of this file.

, the editor SHOULD treat this node and its subtree

is optional: sources backed by reactive stores wire
live updates by calling the listener; static fixtures can omit it
and rely on the sidebar's pull-on-mount semantics.

### Capability gating

Every callback below is **optional**. The sidebar uses capability
detection — "is the callback defined?" — to decide whether to render
the matching affordance. A host that implements

gets the
inline rename input; a host that doesn't gets a plain row. The
existing

gating is the precedent.

### Data-flow rule (PRD §3.3)

**No optimistic mutation in core.** Every callback resolves → host
mutates its registry → host invokes the

: it may return the created page so the UI can
pre-select it before the refresh round-trips.
Return the current page list. May be sync or async.

add-page dialog is submitted. Host creates
the page and (optionally) navigates to it. Async result is
awaited by the dialog so it can show a pending state.

Delete a page. The delete menu item + confirm dialog are hidden
when this callback is undefined OR when

Duplicate a page. Hidden when undefined.

**Optimistic-pre-select exception (PRD §3.3):** the host may
resolve with the created

so the UI can pre-
select it before the standard

is also valid — the row simply
appears once the host's

Rename / re-path a page. The inline rename affordance is hidden
when this callback is undefined OR when

Reorder a page to a new index. Drag handles are inert when this
callback is undefined.
Fired when a page row is clicked. Host routes to that page.

Update page settings + SEO. The settings menu item is hidden
when this callback is undefined; the SEO section inside the
dialog follows the same gate.

Optional subscription. When set, the sidebar calls it on mount

The finite set of field types an AI schema can describe.

Mirrors Puck's own field type union closely but is intentionally a
separate, stable enum — the AI contract must not break every time
Puck adds or renames a field type.

maps Puck
field types to these values during derivation.

Closed union by design: extending this list is a breaking change
to the AI contract.

Theme hint forwarded into AI prompts so the model can pick
appropriate colors, imagery, and tone (review finding TS-4). Named
once here rather than inlining

at each AI context
site. (Kept local to the type-only

layer rather than reusing
the react-layer

, which would cross the engine
boundary.)
Generic bag of format-specific options passed to

.

Declared as a constrained identity type rather than a bare

so format authors can parameterize their
own

signature with a strongly-typed option shape while the
runtime's dispatch layer treats every format's options uniformly.

Format-specific option shape. Defaults to an
unconstrained

so formats that don't care
about options can omit the type parameter entirely.

Severity of a warning produced during an export run.

-

— advisory; nothing wrong, just worth noting.
-

— non-fatal concern (e.g. missing alt text, unused
  prop).
-

— the export succeeded but emitted output the user
  should almost certainly fix (e.g. unresolved asset url). An
  exporter that cannot produce output at all should
Rewrites an asset reference to its resolved location, or returns

when
this resolver does not handle the reference.

Resolvers are registered via the plugin context's

in registration
order. A consuming export format consults each in order and stops at the
first non-null result; how a format treats a reference that no resolver
handles is the format's own policy. May run sync or async — the pipeline
awaits the result.
